cards.title: White House Asks OpenAI to Slow GPT 5.6 Release
cards.body: OpenAI plans to share its newest model GPT 5.6 with select partners instead of broad public release. The Trump administration reportedly raised safety concerns about the rollout.

cards.title: Patronus AI Lands $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: The agent-testing startup, founded by former Meta researchers, raised funding to build digital worlds that stress-test AI systems. Investors cite nearly insatiable market demand for the technology.

cards.title: Claude Gaining Paid Users Against ChatGPT
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over consumers willing to pay for AI despite ChatGPT's dominant market position. Data shows a notable shift in paid user preferences.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game-Trained AI
cards.body: The startup is leveraging millions of hours of gameplay footage to train AI agents for real-world applications. The $2.3B valuation bet reflects confidence that action data develops human-like intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Ex-Chief Eyes 1,000x Power Reduction
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation system tool, demonstrates technology that can replicate conventional AI systems with drastically reduced power consumption. The breakthrough suggests significant efficiency gains are possible.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: The startup provides network switch software to accelerate neocloud deployment timelines. The platform helps operators go live faster with infrastructure optimization.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for Enhancement Tools
cards.body: Adobe announced the acquisition of Topaz Labs, known for image and video enhancement capabilities. The tools will be integrated across Adobe's application suite.

cards.title: Amazon Invests Fresh $13B in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon's latest India investment accelerates its AI infrastructure expansion in the region. The commitment reflects broader tech industry competition for AI capability buildout.

cards.title: Europe Pushes Back on Washington's Chip War
cards.body: ASML's CEO highlighted tensions between U.S. export restrictions and European semiconductor interests. The MATCH Act would limit older-generation chip tool sales already a decade old.

cards.title: Former Infosys Chief Launches New Startup
cards.body: Vishal Sikka's venture, backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, aims to disrupt IT services. The team includes veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

cards.title: Cerebras Stock Plunges After Earnings Report
cards.body: The AI chipmaker's first public earnings forecast narrower gross margins, alarming investors. CEO clarified margin guidance was misunderstood but damage to stock price occurred.

cards.title: Engineering Jobs Prove Most Resilient to AI
cards.body: SignalFire data shows engineers represent a larger share of new hires despite AI disruption narrative. The data contradicts widespread predictions of job losses in technical roles.

cards.title: Top AI Researchers Leave Google for Anthropic
cards.body: Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel joined the exodus from Google to Anthropic. The departures follow earlier moves by Noam Shazeer and John Jumper to rivals.

cards.title: Memory Chip Demand Drives Record Profits
cards.body: A U.S. memory chip company quadrupled revenue to $41.45 billion year-over-year. Profits surged from $1.88 billion to $28.2 billion amid AI infrastructure buildout.

cards.title: Companies Implement AI Budget Token Rationing
cards.body: Organizations are restricting employee AI usage to prevent budget exhaustion on small tasks. The tokenmaxxing era is giving way to token conservation practices.

cards.title: Facebook Launches AI Companion for Creators
cards.body: A new creator app, currently in limited testing, integrates Facebook's AI creator assistant. The tool is designed to support content creators with AI-powered capabilities.

cards.title: Agility Robotics Plans $2.5B SPAC Public Debut
cards.body: The humanoid robotics startup, spun from Oregon State in 2015, will go public via SPAC merger. The deal expects to generate $620 million in proceeds.

cards.title: Figma Adds Code Layers, Animations, AI Features
cards.body: Figma's latest update introduces code layers, motion support, and custom AI-powered plug-ins. The enhancements expand design capabilities for creators and developers.

cards.title: OpenAI Unveils Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI introduced its first custom processor, built by Broadcom and named Jalapeño. The chip was designed specifically for OpenAI's inference system requirements.

cards.title: OpenAI Slows GPT 5.6 Release Over Safety Concerns
cards.body: OpenAI plans to share its newest model GPT 5.6 with select partners instead of the broader public. The Trump administration reportedly asked the company to slow roll the release due to safety concerns.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta researchers, has secured $50M in funding. The company creates digital worlds to stress-test AI agents, experiencing high demand from investors.

cards.title: Claude Gains Ground in Paid AI Consumer Market
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead. Data shows consumers paying for AI are choosing Claude at growing rates.

cards.title: General Intuition Bets $320M on Video Game AI Training
cards.body: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay. The company believes action data from video games can help AI develop human-like intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Ex-Chief Claims 1,000x Power Reduction
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation system, demonstrates how Databricks' technology can replicate conventional AI systems more efficiently. The tool shows potential for dramatically reducing AI's power consumption.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M to Accelerate Neocloud Deployment
cards.body: Netris secured $15M Series A funding from a16z for its network software platform. The company helps neocloud operators reduce deployment time through software running on network switches.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for AI Enhancement Tools
cards.body: Adobe announced its acquisition of Topaz Labs, maker of AI-powered image and video enhancement tools. The tools will be integrated across Adobe's creative applications.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B More in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13B investment in India's AI infrastructure. The move reflects global tech companies racing to expand AI capabilities in the region.

cards.title: Top Google AI Researchers Defect to Anthropic
cards.body: Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, continuing a trend of departures. Previous high-profile researchers Noam Shazeer and John Jumper also recently left Google.

cards.title: Cerebras Stock Plunges After Earnings Forecast
cards.body: AI chipmaker Cerebras saw its stock plunge following its first earnings report since going public. The company's narrower gross margin forecast for its core business spooked investors.

cards.title: Engineers Are Most Resilient to AI Job Displacement
cards.body: New data shows engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, contrary to AI automation fears. Engineers appear to be the most resilient profession despite AI disruption.

cards.title: Companies Ration AI Token Spending After Budget Spikes
cards.body: Firms are implementing token rationing as employees maximize AI API spending on small tasks. The tokenmaxxing era has quickly given way to cost control measures.

cards.title: Facebook Launches AI Companion App for Creators
cards.body: Facebook is testing a new AI companion app with select creators featuring its recently launched creator assistant. The tool is built directly into the app for easier access.

cards.title: OpenAI Unveils Jalapeño Chip Built by Broadcom
cards.body: OpenAI announced its first custom chip, Jalapeño, designed specifically for inference workloads. The processor was built by Broadcom to meet OpenAI's unique system requirements.

cards.title: AI Rapidly Reshapes Behind-the-Scenes Retail Decisions
cards.body: AI is transforming retail not through flashy chatbots but by revolutionizing backend decisions on product placement and inventory. The biggest changes are happening invisibly in how retailers operate.

cards.title: Stripe and OpenAI Back Respiratory Infection Research
cards.body: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are funding efforts to combat respiratory infections like the common cold. The collaboration aims to develop new prevention methods for widespread illnesses.

cards.title: Web Data Infrastructure Layer Emerging for AI
cards.body: Enterprises need data at scale to capitalize on AI, but much relevant information is blocked or unstructured. A new infrastructure layer is emerging to unlock enterprise data for AI models.

cards.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Largest Claude Cloning Attack
cards.body: Anthropic says Alibaba has conducted the largest cloning attack against Claude. The company is calling for punishment related to the AI model duplication.

cards.title: Notion Kills Email App as Users Adopt AI Agents
cards.body: Notion is discontinuing its Skiff-influenced email app due to low user adoption. Most users have shifted to using AI agents instead of the traditional email interface.

cards.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Announce LLM Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled a chip specifically designed for large-language model inference at scale. The processor addresses the computational demands of deploying LLMs in production.

cards.title: IBM Claims World's First Sub-1 Nanometer Chip
cards.body: IBM announced chip technology that could extend Moore's Law another decade with 100 billion transistors in fingernail-sized area. The density doubles previous 2021 state-of-the-art technology.
